summary.scope: Full test design for EPIC-1 — Core Task Platform covering Task CRUD, Real-time Board, and Dependency Graph
summary.approach: Bottom-up: unit tests for repository/service layer, integration tests for API endpoints with Testcontainers, component tests for React UI, E2E for drag-and-drop and real-time flows
objectives: Validate all Task CRUD operations with full field coverage
Verify real-time board updates via WebSocket sync
Ensure dependency graph renders and highlights blocking chains correctly
summary.coverageSummary: 12 total test cases: 4 P0 (critical path), 5 P1 (important), 3 P2 (performance benchmarks)

testLevels.purpose: API endpoint testing with real database

testLevels.coverage: API throughput, board rendering, graph layout

keyDecisions: Use Testcontainers for integration tests to avoid shared staging state
Performance tests run as P2 to avoid blocking releases
Component tests use React Testing Library over Enzyme

riskAssessment.overview: Key risks focus on real-time data consistency and rendering performance at scale

highPriority.description: Concurrent task mutations via WebSocket may cause state divergence between clients
highPriority.testStrategy: Integration test with two WebSocket clients performing simultaneous mutations
highPriority.mitigation: Implement optimistic locking with version fields on task updates

highPriority.description: Drag-and-drop state consistency may break when multiple users interact with the board simultaneously
highPriority.testStrategy: E2E test with Playwright simulating drag operations and verifying final board state
highPriority.mitigation: Server-side status validation on drop events with conflict resolution UI

mediumPriority.description: Graph layout performance degrades significantly with 500+ nodes due to force-directed algorithm complexity
mediumPriority.testStrategy: Performance test measuring layout and render time with 200 and 500 node datasets
mediumPriority.mitigation: Implement node clustering and viewport-based lazy rendering

mediumPriority.description: Query performance with complex filters and large datasets may exceed response time targets
mediumPriority.testStrategy: Load test with k6 using complex filter combinations against 100K task dataset
mediumPriority.mitigation: Add composite database indexes for common filter patterns

lowPriority.description: Basic CRUD operations are well-understood patterns with low risk of failure
lowPriority.testStrategy: Standard unit and integration tests for all CRUD paths
lowPriority.mitigation: Follow established repository pattern with comprehensive input validation

lowPriority.description: Task deletion with soft-delete is a standard operation unlikely to cause issues
lowPriority.testStrategy: Unit tests verifying soft-delete flag and query exclusion
lowPriority.mitigation: Database-level constraint preventing hard deletes in application code

entry.criterion: Test data seeded in staging database
WebSocket server running and accepting connections

exit.criterion: P1 tests passing at target rate

exit.threshold: p95 < 200ms for API, FCP < 2s for board

coveragePlan.overview: Coverage plan organized by priority tiers: P0 covers critical CRUD and real-time paths, P1 covers filtering/rendering, P2 covers performance benchmarks

p0.requirement: Task creation with all required fields
p0.testApproach: Test TaskRepository.create() with valid and invalid inputs
HTTP tests against Fastify server with Testcontainers PostgreSQL
Playwright test: drag task card between columns, verify status update and WebSocket broadcast

p0.requirement: WebSocket real-time sync between two clients
p0.testApproach: Two WebSocket clients connect, one mutates task, other receives update within 500ms

p1.testApproach: Test TaskRepository.findMany() with various filter combinations
Verify soft-delete sets deleted_at, recovery clears it, queries exclude deleted
React Testing Library: render TaskBoard with mock data, verify card presence and positions

p1.requirement: Dependency graph renders nodes and edges
p1.testApproach: Render DependencyGraph with mock tasks, verify SVG nodes and edge connections

p1.requirement: Dependency graph highlights blocking chain on click
p1.testApproach: Playwright: click blocked task node, verify red highlight on blocking chain

p2.testApproach: k6 load test against staging with ramping VUs from 100 to 5000

p2.requirement: Task board renders 500 cards without jank
p2.testApproach: Playwright with performance tracing: load board with 500 tasks, measure FCP and TTI

p2.requirement: Dependency graph renders 200 nodes in < 2 seconds
p2.testApproach: Measure graph layout + render time with 200 interconnected nodes

executionOrder.overview: Tests execute bottom-up: unit first (parallel), then integration (parallel), component (parallel), E2E (sequential), and finally performance (sequential)

parallelization.strategy: Run unit and integration tests in parallel, E2E and performance tests sequentially
constraints: E2E tests require exclusive access to staging environment
Performance tests must run in isolation to avoid resource contention

environments.name: CI
environments.purpose: Automated test execution on every pull request
environments.configuration: GitHub Actions with Testcontainers for PostgreSQL and Redis
environments.dataRequirements: Ephemeral test data created and destroyed per test run

environments.name: Staging
environments.purpose: E2E and performance testing with production-like conditions
environments.configuration: AWS ECS staging cluster with production-like data volume
environments.dataRequirements: Seeded dataset of 100K tasks for performance benchmarks

tools.purpose: Ephemeral database containers for integration tests
